explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 7 from a base nutrition score of 34. Context dragged it down by 27 points.

topPros.title: often inexpensive
topPros.explanation: A practical reason it still exists in real kitchens.

topPros.title: very familiar product
topPros.explanation: People know what it is and how to use it.

topCons.title: processing is a major category negative
topCons.explanation: This is the core reason margarine makes a clean weak anchor in this category.

topCons.title: ingredient simplicity is poor
topCons.explanation: Much weaker trust and wholefood fit than stronger oils/fats anchors.

topCons.title: quality varies wildly by formulation
topCons.explanation: That makes the category fit feel unstable and less trustworthy.

narrationNotes: Context weakened the final tier case by 27 points.

closing.summary: easy and convenient to use and often inexpensive help margarine. but processing is a major category negative and ingredient simplicity is poor drag it down hard. best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only in small amounts when the cooking job matters more than the nutrition case.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
